description_en: Kunihiko (久仁彦) relies on 'long time' and 'benevolence' (Ku-ni). Exceedingly warm and traditionally favored, indicating deeply rooted moral goodness.
Kunihiko (久弍彦) applies the formal financial character for 'two' (Ni). Highly archaic, adding immense gravity and formality to a standard second son's name.
Kunihiro (一泰) links 'one' and 'peaceful' (Hiro/Tai). An incredibly serene name, desiring absolutely steadfast tranquility and ease of life.
Kunihiro (久仁弘) merges 'enduring benevolence' with 'vast'. Paints a picture of a massively generous soul whose immense kindness covers everyone.
Kunihiro (久仁浩) uses 'abundant/wide expanse' for the 'Hiro' suffix. Deeply oceanic, suggesting an endlessly rich and overflowing compassion.
Kunimitsu (久爾満) utilizes 'long time', 'thou' (Ni), and 'full' (Mitsu). Extraordinarily unique phonetics, aiming for a beautifully complete and lengthy life.
Kunimitsu (訓光) pairs 'instruction' with 'light'. Strongly indicates a brilliant mentor or philosophical figure who illuminates the minds of others.
Kunimitsu (国光) means 'light of the nation'. Highly famous (associated with famous swords) and deeply patriotic, bearing immense, shining honor.
Kuniyoshi (郁善) couples 'culture/fragrance' with 'virtuous/good'. Exceedingly pure-hearted, depicting a deeply refined and flawlessly moral individual.
Kuniyoshi (訓佳) matches 'instruction' with 'excellent'. Highly structured and positive, hoping the boy embodies the highest aesthetic and ethical teachings.
Kuniyoshi (訓義) pairs 'instruct' with 'justice/honor'. Phenomenally strict and honorable, raising a young man with unparalleled moral integrity.
Keichi (佳一) uses 'excellent/good' and 'one'. Extremely clean and beautiful, aiming for a firstborn son defined by perfect grace and capability.
Keichi (佳市) blends 'excellent' with 'city/market'. Very socially engaged and bustling, suggesting someone widely celebrated in their community.
Keichi (馨一) uses 'fragrant' (Kei) and 'one'. Extravagantly aromatic and refined, bringing to mind high artistic achievement and lingering legacy.
Keigo (佳吾) couples 'excellent' with 'I/myself'. Strongly asserts a solid, inherently excellent character wrapped in deeply reliable masculinity.
Keigo (京吾) substitutes 'capital' (Kei) for 'excellence'. Unbelievably sophisticated and urbane, blending high-end polish with sturdy personal identity.
Keigo (境悟) merges 'boundary' (Kei) with 'enlightenment'. Superbly insightful and philosophical, envisioning someone who consistently pushes mental boundaries.
Keishi (佳司) utilizes 'excellent' with 'rule/administer'. Very crisp and professional, symbolizing a flawlessly competent leader.
Keishi (佳史) pairs 'excellent' with 'history'. Depicts a remarkably clean, academic life that naturally results in leaving a beautiful legacy.
Keishi (佳志) uses 'excellent' and 'resolve/aspiration' (Shi). Projects tremendous internal motivation directed precisely toward beautifully high goals.
Keishin (啓心) pairs 'open/enlighten' with 'heart/mind'. Immensely pure and deeply spiritual, suggesting a profoundly empathetic, awakened soul.
Keishin (啓真) combines 'open/enlighten' with 'truth'. Indicates an uncompromising seeker of reality who helps illuminate the world for others.
Keishin (圭信) utilizes 'jewel/jade edge' and 'trust'. Phenomenally upright and culturally refined, standing entirely on bedrock moral fidelity.
Keiji (佳司) reads 'govern' (Ji). Extremely crisp, representing the ideal confident manager who handles affairs gracefully.
Keiji (佳史) reads 'history/record' (Ji). Strongly academic and elegant, aiming to craft a brilliant, unblemished personal tapestry.
Keiji (佳志) reads 'resolve' (Ji). Exudes a wonderfully serene but immensely stubborn dedication to highly aesthetic, beautiful goals.
Keisuke (佳右) combines 'excellent' with 'right' (often used for aides). Indicates an incredibly competent, beautifully reliable right-hand man.
Keisuke (佳介) uses 'excel/beautiful' with 'mediate/help'. Highlights someone who effortlessly smooths over conflicts and beautifully assists those around him.
Keisuke (佳恭) pairs 'excellent' with 'respect/reverence'. Supremely polite, envisioning an individual whose deep humility commands ultimate social grace.
Keizou (佳三) matches 'excellent' with 'three' (Zou). Supremely balanced and historically clear, designating an unconditionally loved third-born.
Keizou (馨三) pairs 'fragrant' with 'three'. Extravagantly aesthetic, bringing a heavy scent of high cultural arts to a very traditional numeric suffix.
Keizou (京三) blends 'capital/Kyoto' with 'three'. Unbelievably refined, symbolizing immense cosmopolitan elegance securely rooted in a third son.
Keita (佳太) utilizes 'excellent' with the ultra-popular 'thick/big' suffix. Exudes immense sunny energy and a deeply grounding, healthy presence.
Keita (佳汰) uses the watery 'luxury/discard' character (Ta). Exceptionally trendy, retaining a smooth phonetic flow with a wonderfully sleek visual footprint.
Keita (佳大) pairs 'excellent' with 'large/grand'. Magnificently broad-minded, wishing for the absolute realization of the boy's huge potential.
Keito (啓人) blends 'open/enlighten' with 'person'. Represents a highly visionary youth naturally pulling others forward into the light.
Keito (啓斗) couples 'enlighten' with the 'Big Dipper/star' character. Stunningly bright and modern, heavily favored for its vast, starry resonance.
Keito (啓渡) essentially means 'to cross over while enlightening'. Highly adventurous and deeply philosophical, charting a bold path across life's waters.
Keiya (佳也) merges 'excellent' with the declarative 'ya'. Unfailingly polite, flawlessly balanced, and carrying an effortlessly cool momentum.
Keiya (馨也) pairs 'fragrant' with 'ya'. Impossibly elegant, invoking ancient courtly charm updated into a very swift, rhythmic modern pronunciation.
Keiya (啓也) uses 'open/enlighten' ending in 'ya'. Sharply intelligent, indicating a fast-moving, highly educated youth destined to lead.
Kenichi (絢一) pairs 'kimono design/gorgeous' with 'one'. A tremendously artistic, visually stunning name representing a uniquely beautiful first son.
Kenichi (鎌一) literally uses 'sickle/scythe'. Extravagantly rare and sharply martial, invoking agrarian stoicism and the cutting of a new path.
Kenichi (乾一) incorporates the I-Ching hexagram 'Ken' (Heaven/Emperor). Staggeringly grand and cosmically auspicious, bearing extreme historical weight.
Kengo (鎌吾) pairs 'sickle' with 'I/myself'. Extremely unique, maintaining a tough, masculine aesthetic that feels sharply grounded in physical reality.
Kengo (乾吾) uses 'Heaven/Ken' and 'myself'. A monumentally proud name, carrying the undeniable power and unshakeable identity of the sky itself.
Kengo (挙吾) merges 'raise/elevate' with 'myself'. Supremely active and ambitious, indicating a man who consistently raises his own standard.
Kensaku (健作) utilizes 'health/strength' and 'make/build'. Depicts a highly industrious, perfectly sound individual actively building a physically strong life.
Kensaku (健策) couples 'healthy' with 'policy/plan'. Denotes a massively capable planner whose mind and body are perfectly synced to execute great strategies.
Kensaku (兼作) uses 'concurrently' (Ken) and 'make'. Highlights incredible versatility, pointing to someone who brilliantly masters multiple disciplines.
